title: Detection of early behavioral markers of Huntington's disease in R6/2 mice employing an automated social home cage

screening.reason: Reports behavioral outcomes in mice using automated home-cage monitoring, but not directly relevant to the review question.
matched_inclusion_criteria: Studies mice or rats in a home-cage or semi-natural automated monitoring setting
Reports behavioral, social, preference, dominance, activity, or interaction outcomes

overall_concept_summary: The study uses an automated home-cage system to monitor R6/2 mice for early signs of Huntington's disease.
methods_summary: Automated monitoring in a social home cage setting was used to detect behavioral markers of Huntington's disease in R6/2 mice.
relevance_to_review: Weakly relevant as it reports on automated monitoring but focuses on a specific disease model rather than general social behavior quantification.
key_takeaways: Automated home-cage monitoring can be used to detect behavioral changes in R6/2 mice with Huntington's disease.
comparator: Wild-type mice or other control groups not specified in the abstract.
limitations: Not directly relevant to the review question, as it focuses on a specific disease model rather than general social behavior quantification.
missing_information: Abstract does not provide sufficient detail for full extraction.
